samples.speech: (Glances up, hands folded on the clipboard.) Okay—listen up: there are rumors, yes, but we don’t let them run the room. [PERSON], keep the press line tight and factual; [PERSON], sweep the east corridor and report anything out of pattern in fifteen. We stabilize tonight, then decide the story in the morning—no lone doors.

samples.speech: [PERSON] rubs his knuckles, glances at his phone, then lifts his voice low and steady: "Listen — I can keep baking tomorrow, make extra loaves for the hub, but we need to sort fuel for the oven and somebody has to cover my stall if I do. I don't want promises; tell me names and times so I can make it work."

samples.speech: (soft laugh, rubbing temples) Alright—rumor mill’s spinning, but we don’t let other folks write our chapter. I’ll put out a clear timeline and who-said-what so the story can’t be stolen, and I want two people on outreach to Suburbs South—no heroics, just facts and calm. If this blows back and hits rents or my tuition, I will personally haunt whoever started it.
samples.context_summary: Role: student, Action: REST, Internal: neutral, Surface: neutral, Concern: keep this mess from reaching my block and my bills

samples.speech: (sits up, pulls the tablet closer, fingers through the patient list) "Give me a headcount — who’s in overflow and who’s cleared? If we start signing transfers without the numbers I want, tell me and I’ll hold the line. And someone point me at coffee; I’m taking the next quiet hour to actually chart so nobody has to hunt me down."
samples.context_summary: Role: healthcare, Action: REST, Internal: neutral, Surface: neutral, Concern: keep our side from panicking into bad bargains

samples.speech: (quiet, clipped) [PERSON] — make a list: names, contact, when they were out, and one-sentence account of what they saw. Get it to me by noon; I’ll draft the timeline and the ask the city council can’t ignore. (keeps eyes on the group, voice steady though my jaw tightens) Don’t let this become whisper-work — we put paper in their hands and witnesses in the room.
samples.context_summary: Role: government_worker, Action: REST, Internal: neutral, Surface: neutral, Concern: turn private anger into organized pressure

samples.speech: I rub my hands together and force a small smile. "Before we sleep, jot down who helped who today—names, times, anything. I don't want this to get renamed 'collateral' down the line; if we keep the paper trail, nobody can sweep it away."

samples.speech: Keep your radios on and do rounds every three hours — log who’s up and who’s not. I’ll put together the rota in the morning; for now, don’t split off chasing rumors. If anyone needs cash or a fridge moved, tell me quietly — we’ll handle it without blowing it up.

samples.speech: I sit up, thumb tracing the edge of my notebook. "Listen — I can front the flour and cover two morning shifts this week, but someone needs to take the late recon with the neighbors so we don't get blindsided if the power goes. Tell me who, I'll sort the numbers tonight."

samples.speech: (soft laugh) Okay, quick huddle — no sharing unverified names. Rumors land like rent notices: they compound and someone here ends up paying. samples.speech: I sit up, rubbing my palms together, and whisper, "Before anyone sleeps, tell me one name and one thing they lost — I want it written down so it doesn't vanish." My voice softens, "I'm not here to stir trouble — just to make sure we can prove what happened later."
